behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser/resolver -> dependency graph -> vulnerability-check input handoff across modules (MOCK-MISS yes). REAL-INFRA: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Adversarial critique: if extraction is a single pure function over one normalized structure, Unit could suffice. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require real package-manager subprocess or network resolution; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI model decisions are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner components together with fixture manifests (including transitive chains) and a stub vulnerability client; assert every expected direct+transitive package/version appears in the query/input set.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is contract wiring between range extraction, query construction, and response filtering (MOCK-MISS at module boundary). REAL-INFRA: behavior does not require real internet/API uptime semantics. MOCK-HIDE risk exists if stub is too permissive, so use strict contract fixtures. Adversarial critique: API-specific encoding quirks could require System. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text focuses on correctness of version-range matching, not real HTTP/network behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Wire query builder + version matcher + response mapper against a strict fake API fixture containing same package with multiple vulnerable ranges; assert only CVEs matching manifest range are returned.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Single deterministic ordering rule in local logic; no module handoff required (MOCK-MISS no), no real infra, no AI dependence. Adversarial critique: if sorting is delegated to DB query ORDER BY, Integration might be needed. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because behavior text specifies pure ordering semantics independent of persistence/transport.
behaviors.test_description: Call sort function with mixed severities and equal-severity CVEs with varied CVSS; assert exact output order.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when parser/updater/serializer modules interact; common real bug is unintended reformat/reorder or collateral edits (MOCK-MISS yes). REAL-INFRA: real OS not required; temp local file or in-memory roundtrip is enough. Adversarial critique: if updater is a pure AST transform function, Unit could be enough. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text does not require OS-specific file watcher/locking/subprocess behavior; rejected Agentic/Workflow due no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run parse->update->serialize pipeline on representative manifest fixtures; diff before/after and assert only targeted dependency version token changed.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository/PR side effect and end-to-end git+API behavior (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes). In-process mocks can pass while auth, branch, commit, or diff application fails in real environment. Adversarial critique: a high-fidelity local fake GitHub server could catch much of this, but not the real repo integration guarantee stated here. Rejection-gate: rejected Integration because text says 'created on the real repository'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repository and real PR backend (sandbox org/repo), then assert PR exists and diff contains exactly one dependency version change.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Deterministic semver rule evaluation in local logic, no I/O or multi-module orchestration required for core failure. Adversarial critique: if implementation shells out to package-manager tooling for version interpretation, System could be needed. Rejection-gate: rejected higher levels because behavior text is strictly semver comparison logic.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator with boundary cases (0.x rules as implemented, prereleases, equal versions, major increments) and assert breaking/non-breaking classification.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff: semver decision gates PR module and output reporting module (MOCK-MISS yes). Integration-brake checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess for minimum test, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-involved stages. Adversarial critique: if requirement insists skip on real remote PR service failures/races, System may be needed. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text centers on decision propagation ('breaking change detected' -> 'skip PR' + 'flag output'), testable in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Wire decision engine + PR service interface + report formatter; feed a major-bump case and assert PR client is not invoked and review flag appears in emitted scanner result.
